things.name: Camel Market (Marche au Chameau)
things.description: Hundreds of camels auctioned weekly on the edge of the Sahara Desert

things.name: Banc d'Arguin National Park
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage tidal flats - the world's most important migratory bird staging post

things.name: Iron Ore Train (Mauritania Railway)
things.description: Ride the world's longest and heaviest freight train - 3km of iron ore wagons across the Sahara

things.name: Nouakchott Fishing Port (Port de Peche)
things.description: Hundreds of painted pirogues land their catch - the biggest artisanal fishing port in the world

things.name: Marche Capitale
things.description: Main city market with Tuareg silverwork, indigo cloth and Saharan crafts

things.name: Musee National de Mauritanie
things.description: Mauritanian prehistory, Islamic manuscripts and Moorish silver jewellery

things.name: Chinguetti Ancient Libraries
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage holy city with medieval Islamic manuscript libraries

things.name: Atar & Adrar Plateau
things.description: Dramatic sandstone plateau with palm-lined gorges and ancient caravan towns

things.name: Atlantic Ocean Beaches
things.description: Huge empty Atlantic beaches stretching for hundreds of kilometres

things.name: Thieboudienne (Rice and Fish)
things.description: Shared from Senegal - the definitive West African dish of rice cooked in fish stock with vegetables
